verses.text: Rejoice not, O Israel; for thy harlotry thou shalt not dwell in the LORD's land but eat unclean bread in exile. Joy cut off. Sin forfeits the very land and feasts they boasted in.
Rejoice not, O Israel; for thy harlotry thou shalt not dwell in the LORD's land but eat unclean bread in exile. Joy cut off. Sin forfeits the very land and feasts they boasted in.
Rejoice not, O Israel; for thy harlotry thou shalt not dwell in the LORD's land but eat unclean bread in exile. Joy cut off. Sin forfeits the very land and feasts they boasted in.
Their sacrifices shall not please Him; what will they do in the solemn day when Egypt gathers and Memphis buries them? Worship void. Cast from God's house, their religion is undone.
Their sacrifices shall not please Him; what will they do in the solemn day when Egypt gathers and Memphis buries them? Worship void. Cast from God's house, their religion is undone.
Their sacrifices shall not please Him; what will they do in the solemn day when Egypt gathers and Memphis buries them? Worship void. Cast from God's house, their religion is undone.
'The days of visitation are come'; for their great iniquity the prophet is counted a fool, as in 'the days of Gibeah.' Truth mocked. A corrupt people scorns the very messengers sent to warn them.
'The days of visitation are come'; for their great iniquity the prophet is counted a fool, as in 'the days of Gibeah.' Truth mocked. A corrupt people scorns the very messengers sent to warn them.
'The days of visitation are come'; for their great iniquity the prophet is counted a fool, as in 'the days of Gibeah.' Truth mocked. A corrupt people scorns the very messengers sent to warn them.

God found Israel 'as grapes in the wilderness,' but they went to Baal-peor; now their glory shall fly away, and their children be bereaved. Early promise, sad end. What began in delight is ruined by idolatry.

God found Israel 'as grapes in the wilderness,' but they went to Baal-peor; now their glory shall fly away, and their children be bereaved. Early promise, sad end. What began in delight is ruined by idolatry.

God found Israel 'as grapes in the wilderness,' but they went to Baal-peor; now their glory shall fly away, and their children be bereaved. Early promise, sad end. What began in delight is ruined by idolatry.
Ephraim's children are doomed to the murderer; the prophet can only pray for 'a miscarrying womb.' Grievous judgment. Sin brings sorrow even upon the generations to come.
Ephraim's children are doomed to the murderer; the prophet can only pray for 'a miscarrying womb.' Grievous judgment. Sin brings sorrow even upon the generations to come.

Their wickedness centred in Gilgal; God will drive them from His house and cast them away, for they would not hearken; 'they shall be wanderers.' Homeless. To refuse God's word is to lose His shelter.
